title: Version-compare logic for self-update (no network)
action: Create the src/update module with pure version-compare logic (no network calls in this cell -- fetching is a separate cell). Parse the leading semver triple out of herdr_go::VERSION-shaped strings (format built in build.rs as '{semver} ({sha_segment}, {timestamp})', e.g. '0.1.2 (613e9bd-dirty, 2026-07-22T14:28:03+07:00)', src/lib.rs:19-22 -- the semver is the first whitespace-delimited token) and out of GitHub release tag strings shaped 'vX.Y.Z' (confirmed real tags: v0.1.0, v0.1.1, v0.1.2 -- plain semver, no prerelease/build metadata, so ordinary per-component integer comparison is sufficient, no semver crate needed). Expose a comparison result distinguishing: latest tag equals running version (up to date), latest tag is a higher semver (newer available), latest tag is equal-or-lower (never treat as an update -- per D2 and the plan.md business-logic test-matrix note, a same-or-older tag must never trigger a downgrade).

truths: Comparing running version '0.1.2' against tag 'v0.1.2' reports up-to-date (no update)
Comparing running version '0.1.2' against tag 'v0.1.3' reports a newer version available
Comparing running version '0.1.2' against tag 'v0.1.1' (or 'v0.1.2' again) never reports a newer version available -- equal or lower never triggers an update

artifacts.substantive: exports a version-compare function/type with unit tests covering the three cases above, no TODO stubs

prohibitions: No network calls in this cell
No changes to src/main.rs (CLI verb wiring is a later slice, EP6)

verify: test -f src/update/mod.rs && grep -q '#\[test\]' src/update/mod.rs && cargo test --quiet update

trace.outcome: Added src/update/mod.rs with pure semver parse/compare for D2 (VERSION fingerprint vs vX.Y.Z release tag, no network); wired pub mod update into lib.rs so the module and its tests actually compile

deviations.rule: Implement step 3 (blocking issue in the implementation path)
deviations.description: Cell scope named only src/update/mod.rs as writable, but a new module is invisible to the compiler and to `cargo test` until declared with `mod` in the crate root. Verified empirically: with mod.rs written but undeclared, `cargo test --quiet update` reported "0 tests" and exited 0 (silently vacuous, not a real pass). Added the single line `pub mod update;` to src/lib.rs, mirroring the identical declaration pattern already used for every other top-level module (config, doctor, herdr, notify, security, store, supervisor, watcher, web). No other change made to lib.rs; src/main.rs untouched per the global constraint.
